syscalls.comment:
── Process & thread lifecycle ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  execve/execveat: run the compiled binary (the shell invokes /sandbox/solution).
  clone/clone3/fork/vfork: threading and subprocess creation (std::thread, OpenMP).
  exit/exit_group: normal process termination.
  wait4/waitid/waitpid: parent waits for child threads/processes.
  getpid/getppid/gettid: self-identification, used by runtime libraries.
  getpgid/getpgrp/getsid/setpgid/setsid: process-group management needed by
    shell wrappers and signal delivery.

── File descriptor & I/O ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  read/write/readv/writev/pread64/pwrite64/preadv/pwritev/preadv2/pwritev2:
    all vectored and positional I/O variants needed by libc and user code.
  open/openat/openat2/creat: open files in /sandbox and /tmp (both tmpfs).
  close/dup/dup2/dup3: fd management.
  lseek/ftruncate/truncate/fallocate/fadvise64/readahead: file positioning and
    advisory hints (used by fstream, mmap-backed containers).
  stat/fstat/lstat/newfstatat/statx/statfs/fstatfs: metadata queries needed by
    the C++ runtime, dynamic linker, and any code that opens files.
  fcntl: file descriptor flags (O_NONBLOCK, FD_CLOEXEC), mandatory for pipe
    and socket setup.
  ioctl: required for terminal detection (isatty) and, in the CUDA profile,
    for GPU device communication — kept here so the CUDA profile can inherit.
  sendfile/splice/tee/vmsplice/copy_file_range: zero-copy I/O primitives.
  sync/fsync/fdatasync/msync: data persistence (rarely needed but harmless).
  flock: advisory file locking.
  pipe/pipe2: anonymous pipes (std::process, shell redirects).
  access/faccessat/faccessat2: file-existence checks by the dynamic linker.
  readlink/readlinkat: symlink resolution (needed by /proc/self/exe lookups).

── Memory management ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  brk: heap growth (malloc calls this before falling back to mmap).
  mmap/munmap/mremap: virtual memory mapping and unmapping. Essential for
    dynamic linking, stack growth, std::vector large allocations, and file-backed
    maps. The CUDA profile additionally uses mmap for GPU BAR memory mappings.
  mprotect: change page permissions (needed by JIT compilers and the linker
    when marking text segments executable after relocation).
  madvise: hint the kernel about access patterns (MADV_DONTNEED, etc.).
  mincore: query which pages are resident (used by some allocators).
  mlock/mlock2/mlockall/munlock/munlockall: pin pages in RAM (used by
    cryptographic libraries; unlikely in contest code but harmless).
  memfd_create: anonymous in-memory files (used by some runtimes for
    JIT code emission).

── Signals ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  rt_sig*/kill/tkill/tgkill: signal delivery, masking, and handling.
    Needed for SIGFPE (FP exceptions), SIGSEGV handlers (stack overflow
    detection), and SIGCHLD from forked children.
  sigaltstack: alternate signal stack — required for catching SIGSEGV when
    the main stack has overflowed.
  signalfd/signalfd4: file-descriptor-based signal receipt (used by event loops
    in some runtime libraries).
  pause: sleep until signal arrives (used by simple wait loops).

── Clocks & timers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  clock_gettime/clock_getres/clock_nanosleep: POSIX clocks. Any code that
    benchmarks its own runtime uses these; also used by libc sleep functions.
  gettimeofday/time/times/adjtimex: legacy time interfaces, called by glibc
    and various time-formatting functions.
  nanosleep/alarm/getitimer/setitimer: interval timers.
  timer_create/timer_delete/timer_gettime/timer_settime/timer_getoverrun:
    POSIX per-process timers.
  timerfd_create/timerfd_gettime/timerfd_settime: fd-based timers used by
    event-driven runtimes.

── Scheduler & resource limits ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  sched_*: query/set scheduler parameters and CPU affinity. Used by
    std::thread, OpenMP, and TBB to pin threads to cores.
  getrlimit/setrlimit/prlimit64: query/set resource limits. Some runtimes
    query RLIMIT_STACK on startup to size their initial stack.
  getpriority/setpriority: nice levels.
  getcpu: identify which CPU core is currently executing (used by NUMA-aware
    allocators).

── Credential queries ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  getuid/geteuid/getgid/getegid/getgroups/getresgid/getresuid: identity
    queries. glibc and PAM call these on startup. The container runs as
    nobody (uid 65534) and these must not be blocked or glibc TLS init fails.
  setuid/setgid/setreuid/setregid/setresuid/setresgid/setgroups:
    credential mutations. Allowed so that shell wrappers can drop privileges
    after execve (they have no effect anyway since we run as uid 65534 with
    no capabilities).
  setfsgid/setfsuid: filesystem credential overrides (called by some glibc
    internals; no-op without CAP_SETUID).
  capget/capset: capability queries. glibc reads the bounding set on startup.

── Filesystem mutations ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  mkdir/mkdirat/rmdir/unlink/unlinkat/rename/renameat/renameat2:
    create and remove files inside /sandbox and /tmp (both tmpfs; changes
    are discarded when the container is removed).
  link/linkat/symlink/symlinkat: hard and symbolic links within the sandbox.
  chmod/fchmod/fchmodat/chown/fchown/fchownat/lchown: permission changes
    inside the sandbox (no effect outside the overlay FS layer).
  chdir/fchdir: directory navigation.
  getcwd: current-directory query.
  umask: file creation mask.
  mknod/mknodat: device node creation (allowed inside tmpfs; cannot affect
    the host because no device numbers map to real devices in the container).
  utime/utimes/futimesat/utimensat: timestamp updates.

── Directory iteration ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  getdents/getdents64: iterate directory entries. Used by opendir/readdir in
    glibc (e.g., scanning /proc/self/fd to close extra fds on exec).

── Extended attributes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  [lf]getxattr/[lf]listxattr/[lf]setxattr/[lf]removexattr: xattr operations
    on the overlay FS. Required by some glibc locale and SELinux label lookups;
    harmless inside a tmpfs-backed container.

── IPC — futex & robust lists ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  futex: the fundamental primitive behind all glibc mutex, condvar, and
    semaphore operations. Blocking any thread-based C++ program without this
    is impossible.
  set_robust_list/set_tid_address: per-thread robust futex list, registered
    by glibc's thread startup code.

── IPC — SysV ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  semget/semctl/semop/semtimedop: SysV semaphores (used by some OpenMP
    implementations and legacy parallel code).
  msgget/msgctl/msgrcv/msgsnd: SysV message queues.
  shmget/shmctl/shmat/shmdt: SysV shared memory. Needed by some MPI
    implementations and parallel benchmarks.

── IPC — POSIX message queues ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  mq_open/mq_unlink/mq_timedsend/mq_timedreceive/mq_notify/mq_getsetattr:
    POSIX message queues. Rarely used in contest code but present in some
    parallel C++ libraries.

── Networking (socket layer only — --network none blocks actual traffic) ─────
  socket/socketpair: create socket file descriptors. Even with --network none
    the container can still create AF_UNIX and AF_NETLINK sockets internally;
    glibc's NSS and getaddrinfo code paths attempt socket creation.
  bind/connect/listen/accept/accept4: socket lifecycle (connect will fail
    because the network namespace has no interfaces other than lo).
  getsockname/getpeername/getsockopt/setsockopt/shutdown: socket metadata.
  send/sendto/sendmsg/sendmmsg/sendfile: send data on a socket.
  recv/recvfrom/recvmsg/recvmmsg: receive data from a socket.
  All actual outbound TCP/UDP traffic is impossible because the container
  network namespace has no default route and no external interfaces.

── epoll / poll / select / inotify / eventfd ────────────────────────────────
  epoll_create/epoll_create1/epoll_ctl/epoll_pwait/epoll_wait: I/O event
    multiplexing. Used by async runtimes and some parallel I/O libraries.
  poll/ppoll/select/pselect6: legacy I/O multiplexing; called by glibc's
    stdio implementation when mixing reads and writes on the same fd.
  inotify_*: filesystem event watches (used by some file-watching runtimes).
  eventfd/eventfd2: lightweight inter-thread notifications.

── Async I/O ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  io_setup/io_destroy/io_submit/io_cancel/io_getevents: Linux AIO interface.
    Used by some high-performance I/O libraries as a fallback when io_uring
    is unavailable.

── Process control & misc ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  prctl: process attribute control. Used by glibc to set the thread name
    (PR_SET_NAME) and by some crash handlers to read the dumpable flag.
  seccomp: allows the binary to install its own seccomp filter (additive only
    due to no-new-privileges; cannot weaken the existing filter).
  arch_prctl: x86-64 TLS segment setup. Required by glibc on every thread
    start to set FS_BASE (the thread pointer).
  getrandom: kernel CSPRNG (used by std::random_device, libssl, etc.).
  restart_syscall: kernel-internal syscall used to restart interrupted calls
    after a signal; cannot be blocked without breaking signal handling.
  uname: system name query (some libraries call this to detect kernel version).
  sysinfo: system-wide stats (total memory, uptime). Called by some allocators.
  syslog: userspace syslog interface (unlikely in contest code but harmless).
  ioprio_get/ioprio_set: I/O priority (used by some I/O-intensive runtimes).
  getrusage (via wait4/getitimer path): resource accounting for child processes.

── personality — x86 compatibility layer ────────────────────────────────────
  Allows only the specific values needed for x86/x32 ABI compatibility:
    0          = PER_LINUX (native)
    8          = PER_LINUX32 (32-bit syscall ABI)
    0x20000    = ADDR_NO_RANDOMIZE flag
    0x20008    = PER_LINUX32 | ADDR_NO_RANDOMIZE
    0xffffffff = query-only (returns current persona without changing it)
  Any other value is denied — this blocks attempts to switch to exotic
  personalities (e.g. PER_BSD, PER_SOLARIS) that change syscall semantics.

── Explicitly blocked dangerous syscalls ────────────────────────────────────
  These would be denied by the defaultAction anyway (SCMP_ACT_ERRNO), but
  listing them here makes the intent explicit and allows grep-based audits.

  ptrace           — process inspection / memory injection; potential escape
                     vector. Allowed only in the CUDA profile because the
                     NVIDIA driver requires it for GPU context management.
  bpf              — load eBPF programs; could be used to subvert the host
                     kernel or bypass seccomp itself.
  perf_event_open  — hardware performance counters; enables cache-timing
                     side-channel attacks against co-located workloads.
  mount/umount/umount2 — filesystem mounting; could expose host paths.
  unshare/setns    — namespace manipulation; could escape the container's
                     namespace boundaries.
  kexec_load/kexec_file_load — replace the running kernel.
  init_module/finit_module/delete_module — load/unload kernel modules.
  create_module/get_kernel_syms/query_module — legacy module interfaces.
  pivot_root       — change the root filesystem (container escape vector).
  open_by_handle_at/name_to_handle_at — bypass path-based access controls.
  process_vm_readv/process_vm_writev — cross-process memory access.
  add_key/request_key/keyctl — kernel keyring; could leak secrets from other
                     processes on the host.
  iopl/ioperm      — direct hardware port I/O (x86 only).
  reboot           — reboot or halt the host.
  settimeofday/stime/clock_adjtime — modify system time.
  swapon/swapoff   — add/remove swap devices.
  quotactl         — filesystem quota management.
  kcmp             — compare kernel objects across processes (fd leakage).
  userfaultfd      — user-space page-fault handling; can be used to
                     stall the kernel indefinitely (DoS).
  mbind/set_mempolicy/get_mempolicy/migrate_pages/move_pages — NUMA policy;
                     can cause denial-of-service via memory migration storms.
  nfsservctl/sysfs/lookup_dcookie/uselib/ustat — legacy interfaces.
  vm86/vm86old     — x86 virtual-8086 mode (obsolete, attack surface).
  modify_ldt       — modify local descriptor table (x86 privilege mechanism).
